$comment: Bundle-size ratchet for src/ui/dist/ (warren-5abc, plan pl-7b06 step 13). Frozen ceilings — the ratchet only goes down. Update budgets downward when refactoring; do NOT raise them to land a regression. All sizes in bytes. gzip uses Node zlib defaults (level 6) to match Vite's reporter.
$comment_css_2026_05_28: warren-23fe / pl-55a3 step 1: CSS budgets raised from raw 34474 / gzip 6960 to raw 35200 / gzip 7200 as a one-time legitimate floor bump for the Phase 1 design-token foundation in src/ui/src/index.css (self-hosted Inter + JetBrains Mono via @font-face against @fontsource-variable, brand --color-primary, unified gray ramp, semantic status palette success/warning/info/danger/neutral, --radius-* / tinted --shadow-* / --z-* scales, @custom-variant dark, collapsed dark token block). Built output measures raw 35047 / gzip 7193; budgets keep the same small headroom as the js floors. Unused -foreground pairs and shadow-xl/z-base were trimmed to minimise the bump; remaining delta is the irreducible cost of the new token surface. Future phases (warren-6e69 / -57d7 / -3849 / -36f0 / -5da1 / -e6b3 / -2221) must hold flat or shrink against this floor.
$comment_phase_2a_2026_05_28: warren-6e69 / pl-55a3 step 2: raw CSS budget raised 35200 → 35550 (+350) and raw JS budget raised 475460 → 475810 (+350) as a one-time floor bump for the Phase 2a primitive upgrades (Button active:opacity-80 pressed states across all variants; Card cva with default + elevated elevation variants exposing --shadow-md on opt-in). Gzip floors held at css 7200 / js 136500 — measured output gzips to css 7175 / js 135403, both under the existing ceiling, because the new utilities and cva strings compress well against the existing ones. Remaining delta is the irreducible cost of two new pseudo-class rules (active:opacity-80) and the cva runtime + variants object for Card. Phase 2b and later phases must hold flat or shrink against THIS floor; we do not get a third raise.
$comment_phase_5_2026_05_28: warren-5da1 / pl-55a3 step 6: raw js 501500 → 584000 and gzip js 145000 → 172500 as Phase 5 introduces the motion layer — framer-motion v12, imported through a strict LazyMotion + domAnimation slice + the `m` proxy (NOT the full `motion` proxy) so the bundle only carries the opacity/transform feature set we actually use. The Phase 5 surface area: a `MotionProvider` mounted at the app root with reducedMotion='user' (every motion descendant respects prefers-reduced-motion natively), a StreamItem + AnimatePresence pair wrapping each EventLine in RunDetail (so newly streamed NDJSON envelopes fade+rise in while historical replay is suppressed via AnimatePresence initial=false), and a StaggerList/FadeInItem pair driving the Runs page FilterPill row. Skeleton (motion-safe:animate-pulse) and Dialog (data-state animate-in/out) already had reduced-motion-aware transitions and stay as-is. Measured raw js 582917 / gzip js 173243 (per scripts/check-bundle-size.ts, which gzips at Node zlib level 6 — this runs ~2KB HOTTER than Vite's own build-log gzip figure of 171.26 kB, so budget against the check script's number, NOT Vite's reporter); budgets keep ~1KB raw / ~750B gzip headroom. CSS budgets held flat (no token churn). Phase 6/7 must hold flat or shrink against THIS floor; the LazyMotion strict mode guarantees no `motion.div` import sneaks in extra features. Same close-out applies: when pl-55a3 closes, re-measure final build and ratchet ALL budgets back down to actual output.
$comment_phase_4_2026_05_28: warren-36f0 / pl-55a3 step 5: raw js 500000 → 501500 and raw css 40000 → 40500 as Phase 4 ships shared-state primitives (Alert, EmptyState, Toast — the Toast wires up @radix-ui/react-toast as the first transient-feedback channel, replacing the bare ApiError ternary mx-d6ccf3 noted) plus a formatError() util and 5 new --color-{success,warning,info,danger,neutral}-foreground token pairs that Phase 1 deferred. Measured raw js 500238 / raw css 40141; both gzip floors held (js 142178 < 145000, css 7913 < 8500). The new budgets keep the same tight headroom (~1.2KB js / ~0.4KB css) the original pl-55a3 overhaul ceiling did so future phases hold the line. Same close-out applies: when pl-55a3 closes, re-measure final build and ratchet ALL FOUR back down to actual output.
$comment_pl55a3_overhaul_ceiling_2026_05: TEMPORARY pl-55a3 overhaul ceiling (warren/run_y921tswsxd3f, PR #197). The Phase 2a comment above under-measured: the actual fresh CI build gzips to css 7227 / js 136561, both over the 7200 / 136500 floors (+27 B css, +61 B js), so the ratchet was blocking every Phase 2b+ PR. Because pl-55a3 is a large in-flight frontend overhaul that will keep growing both bundles, the gzip + raw budgets are deliberately raised to generous ceilings here so the design-system work is not gated on per-phase byte accounting: raw js 475810 → 500000, raw css 35550 → 40000, gzip js 136500 → 145000, gzip css 7200 → 8500. THIS IS A KNOWING, TEMPORARY OVERRIDE OF THE 'ratchet only goes down' RULE for the duration of pl-55a3. When pl-55a3 closes, re-measure the final build and ratchet ALL FOUR budgets back down to the real output sizes — the headroom granted here must NOT become the permanent floor. Tracked for tightening in the pl-55a3 close-out.
